paper_title: Antioxidant activity of enzyme-assisted extract derived from round kumquat peel (Fortunella japonica)

entities.id: Antioxidant activity of enzyme-assisted extract derived from round kumquat peel (Fortunella japonica)
entities.description: The main research topic focusing on the capacity to inhibit oxidation using kumquat peel extracts.
The primary field of research involving food processing and chemical engineering.
Lead author and correspondence from Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology.
Co-author from Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology.

entities.id: Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology - Viet Nam National University
entities.description: The academic institution where the research was conducted.
Method using enzymes to break down cell walls to release phenolic compounds.
Commercial pectinolytic enzyme preparation used for extraction.
Experimental design used to evaluate individual variables.
Analytical method used to assess total phenolic content.
Method for measuring antioxidant capacity via cation radical scavenging.
Ferric reducing antioxidant power assay for measuring antioxidant capacity.
Bioactive substances found in plant tissues with antioxidant properties.
Process that decreases shelf life and quality of food products.
Maximum Total Phenolic Content achieved in the extract.

entities.id: ABTS scavenging activity (1993.26±99.66 μmol TE/g dry matter)
entities.description: The peak scavenging capacity measured for the extract.

entities.id: FRAP value (3202.86±160.14 μmol TE/g dry matter)
entities.description: The maximum ferric reducing power achieved.
Percentage of phenolic compounds found in kumquat peel.
Water content of the round kumquat peel.
Solvent used during the extraction process.
